bead.title: drift: bench harness assumes Linux docker --network host without documenting
bead.description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of heimq-9bcd03e1 (commit 0b01249f).
File: heimq/scripts/bench/kafka-perf-test.sh:83-84,90-91,107-108  README: heimq/scripts/bench/README.md (Prerequisites)
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The harness uses 'docker run --rm --network host ...' to reach 127.0.0.1:$HEIMQ_PORT on the host. This works on Linux Docker, but Docker Desktop on macOS/Windows has limited host-networking support: containers cannot routinely reach localhost services on the host even with --network host. README's 'Prerequisites: Docker' does not warn about this. A developer on a Mac will hit a confusing readiness-loop timeout with no clear pointer to the cause.
Suggested fix: pick one of (a) document Linux-only / Docker-Engine-only in the README prerequisites; (b) detect non-Linux and use 'host.docker.internal' as the broker address inside the container while keeping 127.0.0.1 on the host; (c) put heimq into the same docker network as the tooling container. Option (a) is the smallest change and matches the bead's intent (legacy-version path only).
bead.acceptance: Either README.md prerequisites note the platform constraint, or the harness runs successfully on Docker Desktop / macOS with no manual env setup.
